initial_confirmation.note: user requested fully autonomous execution; no gates

tasks.subject: M1 — Skeleton: HTML shell, tokens, fonts, theme toggle, uppertab routing
M2 — Landing uppertab: About + Methodology + Collaboration
M5 — Map enhancements: multi-select union, counter-scaled zoom, N/S explainer
M6 — Polish, footer, accessibility, dev server launch

decisions.decision: use uv-managed local .venv for the Python build script
vendor D3 v7 + TopoJSON v3 locally under src/scripts/lib/

top-5 catalytic cases = data-driven from total citation count, filtered by catalytic_exclusions.json (EU procedural cases excluded). Resulting top-5: Urgenda, Shell v NL, Mass v EPA, Neubauer, KlimaSeniorinnen.
no bundler / no minifier for v1

use `inert` (not `hidden`) on non-active uppertab panels so the horizontal flex track keeps its 300vw layout while the translate-X actually slides into view. Fixed a layout collapse bug where applying hidden to siblings shrunk the track and pushed the active panel off-screen.
headless-Chrome smoke tests via DOM dump + screenshot used as the primary verification harness during implementation (no separate test framework)

headless_smoke_results.map_multi_select_brazil_argentina_outgoi: 47 citations / 11 countries / 24 arcs — matches expected union semantics

spec_gaps: spec lists 5 named catalytic cases (Urgenda, Neubauer, Mass v EPA, Leghari, Notre Affaire) but also requires data-driven top-5; the raw data ranks differently, so the build script applies a small exclusion list for EU procedural cases. See decisions d-003.
spec doesn't define a stable centroid table for jurisdictions; we ship build/country_centroids.json as a small hand-curated table.
bugs_discovered_and_fixed: navigation: onNavigate listener was registered AFTER initNav's initial emit — fixed by reordering bootstrap.
layout: hidden attribute on non-active uppertab panels collapsed the flex track and slid the active panel off-screen — fixed by switching to inert + aria-hidden.
